description: §6.2 Shape 4 refinement-violating-empty (codes): a refinement-typed no-RHS cell whose base canonical empty VIOLATES the predicate cannot be auto-defaulted. `<x>: number(>0)` — base empty 0 fails `>0` — fires E-REFINEMENT-NO-DEFAULT (§53/§34); an explicit initializer is required. (impl also surfaces the companion E-CONTRACT-001; asserted via superset, only E-REFINEMENT-NO-DEFAULT is pinned.)
rationale: §6.2 Shape 4: a refined type with no predicate-satisfying canonical empty is a hard error (E-REFINEMENT-NO-DEFAULT). The check is compile-time: the §53 predicate is statically evaluated on the literal canonical-empty (0), which fails >0.
